mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at or near cycle-high levels (FEDFUNDS >= 5.0%) for multiple consecutive months keeps the equity discount rate elevated, compressing forward-looking valuations and producing below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods of falling or low rates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% since mid-2006 through Feb 2007
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns during months with FEDFUNDS >= 5.0% are not statistically lower (t-test p > 0.05) than returns during months with FEDFUNDS < 4.0%, or rank IC stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) signals tightening credit conditions and weakening consumer wealth effects that transmit to broader demand with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter once the decline exceeds a material threshold.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls more than 20% below its trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts vintage_value near 1408K in Jan 2007, down substantially from 2005-2006 peaks above 2000K
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following a >20% HOUST drawdown are positive or indistinguishable from unconditional baseline in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of rising UNRATE alongside decelerating PAYEMS growth signals an inflection in labor market strength that precedes broad earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the co-movement is required to distinguish noise from genuine labor turning points.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% and PAYEMS growth moderating in early 2007
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met jointly, forward 20-day S&P returns are positive in more than 55% of instances across the 2005-2024 sample, or the interaction signal's rank IC does not exceed the sum of each single-series IC by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real-time strength in goods-producing sectors that is quickly priced into equities, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets extrapolate near-term earnings momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 111.93 as of Jan 2007 reflecting continued expansion
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO month-over-month prints are not significantly higher (p > 0.05) than returns following negative prints across a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains above the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate by a wide margin (real rate high and restrictive), tight monetary conditions progressively slow demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter once the restrictive threshold has persisted.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate exceeds 2.5pp for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% vs CPI inflation near 2.0-2.5% in early 2007 implying elevated real rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of real-rate restriction >2.5pp are not significantly lower than the unconditional sample mean, or the effect reverses sign in more than one of three rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) during a late-cycle expansion confirms resilient consumer demand, supporting continued equity appreciation over the next month; the same signal loses predictive power or reverses once the expansion transitions into contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 370,495M in Feb 2007 consistent with steady consumer spending
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth periods during late-cycle expansion fail to produce forward 20-day returns exceeding the unconditional sample mean at p < 0.10, or the sign flips to negative in a recessionary subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A material deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth below a critical threshold signals an approaching labor market turning point that historically precedes broad equity market weakness with a lag of roughly one quarter, as consumption and corporate profit expectations adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 137,410K in Feb 2007 with growth moderating from 2005-2006 pace
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following a sub-0.8% PAYEMS growth trigger are positive or flat in more than 55% of historical instances, invalidating the lead-lag relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production (INDPRO) continues rising while housing starts (HOUST) fall sharply indicates late-cycle imbalance between manufacturing momentum and interest-rate-sensitive demand, foreshadowing a broad growth slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month once the divergence widens beyond a threshold.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth positive while HOUST 3-month growth below -10%
evidence_refs: fact:Early 2007 shows continued industrial output growth alongside sharply declining housing starts
regime_hint: late-cycle divergence between manufacturing and housing sectors
failure_condition: When the INDPRO-up/HOUST-down divergence trigger fires, forward 20-day S&P returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction signal's incremental rank IC over either single-series signal is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
